title: Is there a way to view when a user was deactivated?
excerpt: Just as the title says. I’d like to be able to view a user’s deactivation date. Is there a way to do this?

posts.author: klewis (Karli Lewis)
posts.cookedHtml: Just as the title says. I’d like to be able to view a user’s deactivation date. Is there a way to do this?

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes you can view them in the users:

  1. Click the Account thumbnail in the right corner.
  2. Click on the Admin Option in the drop down.
  3. Click on the Users Tab in the Navigation tab on the left side of the screen

All the users along with their joining and last activity date will be available there.

Zaki

Business Workflow Consultant

Ready Logic

posts.author: klewis (Karli Lewis)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, thank you for your answers, but I would like to know if there is a way to view/capture the date of deactivation. While the “last active” is helpful, it is not the same thing as deactivation date.

posts.author: robertor (Roberto)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Karli,

Yes, you can see it if you are an admin.

Go to the Administration section. Under Security look for the Audit tab.

There you can filter by Event Type.

The report will tell you When the user was deactivated and who did it. I hope this is the information you are looking for.

posts.author: jrhams (Jeff)
posts.cookedHtml:
Administration → Security → Audit → Event Type
